name: Sause v. Bauer
question: Does the failure of the self-represented plaintiff in this case to raise her Fourth Amendment claim on appeal after bringing closely related First and Fourth Amendment claims in the original action render unnecessary the court’s resolution of her Fourth Amendment claims?
description: A case in which the Court held that a self-represented woman's failure to raise a Fourth Amendment claim on appeal, when her original complaint raised "inextricable" First and Fourth Amendment issues, did not obviate the need for the lower court to resolve the Fourth Amendment issues.
